data.description:

An announcement from SIIC Environment Holdings ( (SG:BHK) ) is now available.

SIIC Environment Holdings has scheduled its 2026 annual general meeting for 29 April 2026 at Conrad Singapore Marina Bay, where shareholders from both Singapore and Hong Kong are invited to attend in person and vote or appoint proxies. The company will distribute its FY2025 annual report, AGM circular, notice, and proxy form primarily via electronic channels, while offering printed materials only on request, in a move that underscores its sustainability push and aligns with market trends toward digital communication and enhanced shareholder engagement, including structured pre-meeting Q&A and published AGM minutes.

More about SIIC Environment Holdings

SIIC Environment Holdings is a Singapore-incorporated company listed in both Hong Kong and Singapore, operating in the environmental services sector. The group focuses on water and wastewater treatment and related environmental infrastructure, serving municipal and industrial clients across its key Asian markets.

data.title: Tong Ren Tang Technologies Proposes Final 2025 Dividend and Outlines Tax Treatment
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Tong Ren Tang Technologies Co ( (HK:1666) ).

Tong Ren Tang Technologies Co. Ltd. has announced a proposed final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.18 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with payment scheduled for 11 August 2026, subject to shareholder approval. Key timetable details, including ex-dividend date, record date, and the default payout currency, will be announced later, and Computershare Hong Kong Investor Services will serve as the share registrar.

The company has detailed the withholding tax treatment that will apply to H-share investors based on their residency and investment channel, including a 10% corporate income tax for non-resident enterprise shareholders, a 10% individual income tax for most non-resident individuals, and a 20% individual income tax for investors accessing H shares via Shenzhen-Hong Kong Stock Connect. These tax arrangements clarify the net dividend investors can expect and underscore the company’s adherence to PRC tax regulations, while explicitly disclaiming responsibility for individual shareholders’ tax status or any disputes over withholding mechanisms.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1666) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tong Ren Tang Technologies Co stock, see the HK:1666 Stock Forecast page.

More about Tong Ren Tang Technologies Co

Tong Ren Tang Technologies Co. Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in the manufacture and sale of pharmaceutical products, with its shares traded under stock code 01666. The company issues H shares to both resident and non-resident investors and is subject to Mainland Chinese tax rules on dividend distributions to different categories of shareholders.

Average Trading Volume: 1,146,049

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

The latest update is out from Termbray Industries International (Holdings) Limited ( (HK:0093) ).

Zero Fintech Group Limited reported audited consolidated revenue of HK$359.5 million for 2025, up from HK$261.8 million a year earlier, driven by growth in its core financial services business. Despite a fair value gain on investment property and higher other income, increased advertising, promotion and employee expenses heightened the group’s cost base.

Profit attributable to equity holders fell to HK$20.9 million from HK$27.1 million, as impairment losses on loan and interest receivables climbed sharply to HK$147.1 million and finance costs rose to HK$24.5 million. Basic and diluted earnings per share declined to 0.78 HK cents, although total comprehensive income improved to HK$27.9 million on favourable property revaluation and foreign exchange translation gains.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0093) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Termbray Industries International (Holdings) Limited stock, see the HK:0093 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Bank of Communications Plans RMB14.88 Billion Cash Dividend for 2025
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Bank of Communications Co ( (HK:3328) ).

Bank of Communications Co., Ltd. has approved an annual profit distribution plan for 2025, proposing a cash dividend of RMB1.684 per 10 shares, based on total share capital of 88,363,784,223 ordinary shares as of 31 December 2025. The planned payout amounts to RMB14.88 billion and will be distributed to both A-share and H-share holders, subject to approval at the 2025 annual general meeting on 26 June 2026.

The bank intends to keep the total cash dividend amount unchanged even if its share capital changes before the record date, adjusting the per-share dividend accordingly and announcing any revisions separately. If shareholders approve the plan, A-share investors of record on 9 July 2026 are expected to receive payment on 10 July, while H-share holders of record on the same date are slated to be paid on 14 August 2026, with the H-share register closing from 4 to 9 July for entitlement processing.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3328) stock is a Buy with a HK$7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bank of Communications Co stock, see the HK:3328 Stock Forecast page.

More about Bank of Communications Co

Bank of Communications Co., Ltd. is a major commercial bank incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong, providing a broad range of banking and financial services. It serves both domestic and international customers through A shares and H shares, reflecting its dual-market presence and role in China’s banking sector.

data.title: Sichuan Baicha Baidao Tightens Audit Committee Rules to Bolster Governance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sichuan Baicha Baidao Industrial Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2555) ) just unveiled an update.

Sichuan Baicha Baidao Industrial has formalised detailed terms of reference and procedural rules for its Audit Committee, mandating that it comprise at least three non-executive directors, the majority of whom must be independent and include at least one member with professional accounting or financial expertise. The committee will meet at least twice a year, keep comprehensive minutes, report to shareholders at the annual general meeting and is supported by the joint company secretary or another qualified appointee.

The revamped framework strengthens the Audit Committee’s authority over the appointment, remuneration and oversight of external auditors, while also clarifying its mandate to monitor financial reporting, risk management and internal control systems. By reinforcing independence requirements, restricting recent partners of the audit firm from serving and ensuring direct engagement with auditors without executives present, the company is signaling a tighter governance structure designed to enhance transparency and investor trust.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2555) stock is a Hold with a HK$7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sichuan Baicha Baidao Industrial Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2555 Stock Forecast page.

Sichuan Baicha Baidao Industrial Co., Ltd. Class H operates in the corporate sector where robust governance and oversight of financial reporting, risk management and internal controls are central to stakeholder confidence. The company relies on a board structure with non-executive and independent non-executive directors to supervise audit and compliance functions in line with market and regulatory expectations.

data.title: SSY Group Profit Halves as Revenue and Margins Slide in Challenging 2025
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SSY Group ( (HK:2005) ) has issued an announcement.

SSY Group reported a sharp deterioration in 2025 results as shrinking demand, tighter policies and intensifying competition hit its pharmaceutical business, with revenue falling 27.8% to about HK$4.17 billion and net profit dropping 55.6% to HK$471 million. The revenue decline was broad-based across key infusion and injection product lines, gross margin contracted by 8.8 percentage points to 41.6%, and the board cut the full-year dividend by 54.3% to HK$0.08 per share, underscoring the earnings pressure and a more cautious capital return stance toward shareholders.

Management responded by emphasizing an integrated “bulk pharmaceuticals + preparations” strategy and efforts to reinforce dominant products amid a rapidly evolving drug market, though the data highlight that SSY is facing its most severe operational headwinds in recent years. The results signal a tougher competitive and regulatory landscape for hospital-use infusion and injectable drugs in China, with implications for margin sustainability and the group’s ability to restore growth and maintain investor returns in the near term.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2005) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SSY Group stock, see the HK:2005 Stock Forecast page.

More about SSY Group

SSY Group Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated pharmaceutical group listed in Hong Kong, focusing on intravenous infusion solutions, bulk pharmaceuticals, ampoule injections and oral preparations. Its product portfolio is geared toward hospital and medical market demand in China and related markets, with a core strategy of integrating bulk drug production with finished dosage forms.

Average Trading Volume: 8,346,836

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Hanx Biopharmaceuticals Raises Caps on Connected Raw Material Purchases
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Hanx Biopharmaceuticals (Wuhan) Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3378) ) is now available.

Hanx Biopharmaceuticals plans to raise the annual caps on its raw material purchases from connected party Waterstone Pharmaceuticals, reflecting higher-than-expected demand for small molecules used in its ADC development. The company now intends to lift the caps under the Raw Materials Supply Framework Agreement to RMB6.5 million for 2026 and RMB5.0 million for 2027 after reassessing its needs.

Because Waterstone is controlled by the same major shareholder as Hanx, the higher caps constitute continuing connected transactions under Hong Kong listing rules, triggering reporting, annual review and announcement obligations. However, as the relevant percentage ratios remain below 25% and the caps are under HK$10 million annually, the revisions are exempt from circular issuance and independent shareholder approval requirements.

More about Hanx Biopharmaceuticals (Wuhan) Co. Ltd. Class H

Hanx Biopharmaceuticals (Wuhan) Co., Ltd. is a pre‑revenue biopharmaceutical company listed in Hong Kong and focused on developing antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) products. The group relies on external partners such as Waterstone Pharmaceuticals to supply key small-molecule raw materials supporting its research and development pipeline.

Hanx Biopharmaceuticals reported its audited annual results for 2025, posting a wider loss of RMB131.5 million as R&D and administrative expenses increased alongside higher finance costs, despite a rise in other income and gains. data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Country Garden Services Holdings Co ( (HK:6098) ) has provided an update.

Country Garden Services Holdings Company Limited has declared a special dividend of RMB 0.418 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholders able to receive the payout in cash by default or elect a scrip share option. Key dates include an ex-dividend date of 8 June 2026, a record date of 11 June 2026, and an expected payment and share certificate dispatch date of 21 August 2026.

The board indicated that if the company’s total number of issued shares changes before the 11 June 2026 record date, the dividend per share may be adjusted to keep the total payout at no less than 60% of core net profit attributable to owners, approximately RMB 1,511.4 million. This approach signals a commitment to maintaining a substantial shareholder return policy while providing flexibility through a scrip option that can support capital structure management and potential reinvestment by investors.

data.title: Country Garden Services Lifts Revenue and Dividends Despite Sharp Profit Drop in 2025
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Country Garden Services Holdings Co ( (HK:6098) ) has provided an update.

Country Garden Services reported a 9.9% rise in 2025 revenue to RMB48.35 billion, driven by growth in property management area, while gross profit was broadly flat and core net profit attributable to owners fell 17.1% to RMB2.52 billion. Headline net profit slumped 68.1% to RMB598.9 million, but the group cut its general and administrative expense ratio, maintained a strong cash position of RMB17.93 billion, raised total dividends to RMB1.51 billion, and executed a sizeable share repurchase and cancellation of nearly 88 million shares, signalling confidence despite profit pressure.

The group’s revenue-bearing GFA from property management excluding the “Three Supplies and Property Management” segment increased to 1,070.4 million square meters, while the “Three Supplies and Property Management” GFA edged down to 88.8 million square meters, indicating a shift in business mix. The combination of higher dividends, reduced costs and ongoing buybacks suggests management is prioritising shareholder returns and operational efficiency, even as reported earnings come under strain from one-off and non-core items reflected in the gap between net and core profit.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6098) stock is a Hold with a HK$7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Country Garden Services Holdings Co stock, see the HK:6098 Stock Forecast page.

More about Country Garden Services Holdings Co

Country Garden Services Holdings Company Limited is a China-based integrated property management and related services provider, listed in Hong Kong. The group manages large-scale residential and non-residential projects, including traditional property management and “Three Supplies and Property Management” businesses, with over 1.1 billion square meters of revenue-bearing gross floor area under management.

MTT Group Holdings Limited has provided a further update on the winding-up petition previously disclosed, stating that the agreed settlement amount represents about 5% to 6% of the group’s unaudited consolidated total assets as of 30 September 2025. China Shineway Pharmaceutical Group Limited ( (HK:2877) ) has issued an announcement.
